summary: Gruber announces a new display ad format for Daring Fireball to replace The Deck Network, which closed in March 2017. The format sells up to 5 sponsor slots per month, each receiving 20% of page views, with no JavaScript, no tracking, and images served from daringfireball.net itself. He argues this arrangement benefits sponsors (sole graphical presence on a minimal page), readers (privacy-respecting, fast-loading), and himself (sustainable revenue with a clear conscience). He also challenges the ad industry's reliance on third-party tracking, arguing advertisers should measure their own performance via custom URLs rather than trusting platform-reported numbers.
key_points: The new ad format caps at 5 sponsors per month, each getting 20% of page views, with no JavaScript or tracking involved — just images, text, and HTML links
DF's minimal visual design means the ad is often the only graphical element and only color on the entire page, making it noticeable without being obtrusive
Gruber argues advertisers shouldn't trust platform-reported ad metrics and should instead use custom URLs to independently measure performance
The approach prioritizes reader privacy as a core feature, with Gruber pledging to publicly announce any future change to that policy
Longtime supporters like Rogue Amoeba, Field Notes, MailChimp, and Jamf were the first sponsors, continuing a pattern of direct advertiser relationships
money_quotes: The ads on Daring Fireball stand out without being the least bit obtrusive. That's the sweet spot for ads, in my opinion.
Not only is there no tracking involved, there is no JavaScript involved. They're just images, text, and HTML links.
You have my word that I will never allow tracking via these or any other ads on Daring Fireball without posting a big prominent 'OK, DF Ads Are Now Tracking You' post on the site.

If you pay (say) Facebook for an ad, why in the world would you, the advertiser, trust Facebook's numbers for how the ad performed? Measure the performance yourself, and you'll know you're getting the truth.
If you want to support DF, just take a look at the ads, and when one genuinely seems interesting to you, check it out. That's all I need.
I can sleep soundly at night because the ads (a) look good, (b) load super fast, and (c) respect the privacy of DF readers.
key_insight: Advertising can be effective and sustainable without surveillance technology — minimal, well-designed ads on a trusted site stand out precisely because they respect both the reader's attention and privacy.
